text: Developing a mobile app requires careful planning. One key concept is responsive design, which ensures the app looks and works well on different screen sizes, from phones to tablets. Developers must choose between native and cross-platform approaches. Native apps are built specifically for iOS or Android, offering better performance and smooth integration with device features. In contrast, cross-platform tools like React Native allow a single codebase for both systems, saving time but sometimes compromising performance. Once ready, the app is uploaded to app stores like Google Play or the Apple App Store, where quality and security standards must be met. Ultimately, a well-designed app focuses on user experience and consistent performance.
